_comment: Gameplay/UI event -> SFX-asset-id -> one-shot mapping for the SFX trigger runtime (AUD-01 seed, issue #148). The SFX counterpart of zone_music_config.json (#144): music resolves zone->set->stem, SFX resolves event->sfx-id->one-shot through the SAME content-ID hook (music SAD §5). Content references stable asset IDs ONLY (music PRD §7) — never file paths. The runtime datastore (IF-5 pack manifest, PackEntry.resource) resolves each asset ID to a res://meridian/... resource. At M0 the resolver is the PLACEHOLDER factory (sfx_placeholder_factory.gd) — synthesized clicks/blips, NOT real sound effects. Real SFX authoring (#143 audio direction) is deferred.

categories._comment: category -> {bus, concurrency group, cap}. Bus tree per music SAD §2.5; group + cap per PRD §3.4. cap is carried as data for the M1 voice manager — NOT enforced at M0 (the M0 system is routing + trigger only, no voice manager yet).

sfx._comment: ~10 PLACEHOLDER one-shots — procedurally synthesized enveloped tones per entry (short attack, fast decay, NON-looping). Clearly not real SFX; they prove the event->id->one-shot trigger system + the ID hook path end-to-end without shipping any audio asset. placeholder_tone_hz / placeholder_ms drive the synth in sfx_placeholder_factory.gd.

events._comment: Client-authored event registry (music SAD §5): events with no content object. Footsteps: physics-material -> surface tag -> foley footstep variation group. UI: UI event registry -> sfx.ui.*. Login sting: the boot/login one-shot. Content-carried ids (ability cast_sfx/impact_sfx, npc sound_set) are NOT listed here — they carry their own sfx.* id in /content and resolve through the SAME resolved_sfx() hook.
